rationale: The only substantive ground-truth concern (yJader's note that already_submitted shouldn't gate the diffusion singleton-list unwrap since both submit paths funnel into the same DiffusionEngine call) is addressed by both, but X reconstructs the same reasoning with grounded citations (stage_pool.py:951/1022 raising ValueError on both paths), while Y just asserts consistency without the underlying why. X also correctly identifies 'return outputs' vs 'DiffusionOutput(output=raw)' as a strict improvement, backed by a specific citation showing ZImagePipeline's actual return construction (pipeline_z_image.py:720), whereas Y flags the same change as a 'major' risk needing confirmation based on vague, uncited claims about request_id/finished fields — an unsubstantiated and inflated-severity finding that hurts its precision. Both give file:line-anchored, actionable suggestions (regression test), but X's verdict (APPROVE) also matches the actual outcome while Y's 'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' is a needless overcall.
